minimal_context: The student is practicing catching a ball while standing on a spot in the gross motor area.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We finished jumping. Let's check the picture schedule. It says catch. Please stand on the spot.

STUDENT: (Smiles and jumps up and down, looking really excited) Catch.

TUTOR: (Kneels about 2 feet in front of the student) Hands out, look at the ball.

STUDENT: (Catches the ball with two hands, then squeezes the ball)

TUTOR: Okay, let's do that again.

STUDENT: (Bounces on the ball, makes high pitch noises and flaps hands)

options.A: Say "Whose turn?" as a verbal prompt during the session to redirect the student back to the ball-catching activity.
options.B: Elicit the unhelpful thought driving the behavior, then help the student recognize how their actions impact the tutor's willingness to continue catching.
options.C: Say "Jump it out," holding the student's hands as they jump up and down to incorporate their movement.
options.D: Delay intervening straight away during the activity, but carefully control the duration of the bouncing and hand-flapping incident.
